title: Iranian regime scrambles to survive after Khamenei's death
subhead: Amid scenes of celebration and mourning, the formation of an interim government and ongoing bombings, Iran has entered a period of profound uncertainty, where the Islamic Republic's very survival and the reshaping of its internal balance of power are at stake.

title: Israel unites against the 'existential threat' of Iran: 'We need to get rid of this regime that hates us'
subhead: With schools, non-food shopping centers and restaurants closed, Israelis have been living since Saturday under air raid alerts and Iranian missile strikes. These attacks have already claimed several lives.

title: Europeans ready for defensive military actions in region after Iranian strikes in the Gulf
subhead: French President Emmanuel Macron, German Chancellor Friedrich Merz and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said they were 'appalled by the indiscriminate and disproportionate missile attacks' launched by Iran in response to the US-Israeli offensive.

title: French nuclear 'umbrella' for Europe now increasingly discussed among allies
subhead: French President Emmanuel Macron will deliver a speech on nuclear deterrence on Monday, with Europeans expected to pay close attention amid threats from Russia and growing doubts over the United States' reliability as a NATO ally.

title: American press voices concerns over Trump's operation in Iran: 'A reckless, imperial error'
subhead: Many columnists approved of the death of Iran's supreme leader on Saturday, but questioned the future of the regime and the region, with some lamenting the lack of Congressional consultation before launching the war.

Attacks on Iran: US and Israel are committing 'a crime of aggression,' says Bernie Sanders' former adviser
subhead: An influential voice in the progressive camp and former advisor to Senator Bernie Sanders, Matthew Duss harshly criticizes Donald Trump's actions following Saturday's strikes, stating that 'this war is unfortunately the culmination of decades of a militarist foreign policy.'

title: David W. Bates, cybernetics specialist: 'The brain is not a computer'
subhead: With the advent of artificial intelligence, the American academic aims to avoid the pitfalls of both technophobia and transhumanism. In his view, it is necessary to move beyond a simplistic opposition between 'human' or 'natural' intelligence and AI.

title: EU-Mercosur trade deal set to take effect without waiting for European Parliament vote
subhead: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen fast-tracked the announcement on Friday, much to France's dismay.
